countries/mali.summary: Joint JNIM-FLA forces ambushed a Malian army convoy leaving Anefis on Jul 18, killing at least 50 pro-government forces; Malian army claims ~20 militants killed in retaliatory strikes. Follows the Jul 4-10 six-day siege/retaking of Anefis (~30 soldiers killed).

countries/democratic-republic-of-c.summary: DRC Ebola (Bundibugyo strain) death toll reached 930 (2,344 confirmed cases) as of data through Jul 18, up from 600 (Jul 9) and 500 (Jul 6) — the fastest-growing Ebola outbreak on record, with dozens of health workers among the dead. Ituri province worst-hit (776 deaths).

countries/chad.summary: Lake Chad Basin violence up 80% since 2024 (nearly 1,800 incidents, 5,700+ fatalities Sept 2025-May 2026); Chad hosts 2.26M displaced; $1.4B response plan only 9% funded. No new dated incident confirmed within the Jul 7-21 window.

UN OHCHR verified 65,044 civilian casualties in Ukraine since the invasion, with a sharp escalation in H1 2026.

countries/yemen.summary: 73 UN/humanitarian staff remain in Houthi detention, restricting aid to ~70% of need areas; over 18M people acutely hungry. No specific fresh in-window (Jul 7-21) dated escalation was independently confirmed this cycle beyond sustained conditions.

countries/syria.summary: Syrian Network for Human Rights met with UN Deputy Special Envoy Jul 15 on transitional justice; EU announced a EUR 175M package for public-institution and accountability support. Severe drought continues to worsen the humanitarian crisis.

countries/libya.summary: Frozen conflict/political-crisis status persists; rival factions met in Sirte in July but no new kinetic incident or rights violation was confirmed within the Jul 7-21 window.

countries/sudan.summary: European Parliament voted 476-28 to call for RSF terrorist designation over the siege of El Obeid (~500,000 trapped, including 105,000 displaced); city without electricity for nearly a month, bombed water infrastructure, growing famine fears. UN OHCHR warns of impending "catastrophe."

countries/somalia.summary: UN confirms "a real and credible risk of famine" in Barakaba district, South West state (report Jul 16); ~6M people face critical food insecurity, nearly 2M children acutely malnourished, ~500,000 in need of urgent treatment; 2026 Humanitarian Response Plan only 15.2% funded.

countries/eritrea.summary: Ethiopia-Eritrea war risk sustained at elevated levels; no confirmed new kinetic incident dated within the Jul 7-21 window this cycle.

countries/afghanistan.summary: Human Rights Watch published a dedicated report (Jul 15) raising continued civilian-casualty concerns over Pakistani strikes in Afghanistan, following Taliban's Jul 1 first-ever drone strikes into Pakistani territory. Q1 UNAMA baseline: 372 civilians killed, 397 injured.

countries/north-korea.summary: Citizens' Alliance for North Korean Human Rights marked its 30th anniversary (Jul 9) reviewing forced-labor supply-chain disclosure work; an advocacy/commemorative event rather than a new documented abuse escalation.

countries/myanmar.summary: ACLED's 100,114 post-coup death-toll milestone was reported Jul 1-2, just before this scan's 14-day window; no new dated mass-casualty event was confirmed inside the Jul 7-21 window this cycle. Logged as sustained/background only.
